strengths: 15+ years of polyglot fullstack delivery across TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, Docker, and Kubernetes — well exceeds the 6-year minimum and covers most of the primary stack
Current role explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework development, directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement
Demonstrated software craftsmanship culture through TDD advocacy, open-source test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and community workshops — signals code quality and collaboration fit
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume despite it being a named primary framework for the role
Vitest and Playwright are absent from the listed skills and project history; testing experience appears rooted in DLang and custom runners rather than the required JS/TS testing ecosystem

MIT Computer Science degree dates (2009–2012) overlap with simultaneous employment in multiple cities across two continents, which warrants verification before drawing on this credential as evidence of formal CS grounding
response.justification: Bogdan brings substantial fullstack depth and a hands-on AI-agent background that aligns well with the role's emerging-technology expectations, and his track record of owning end-to-end features across varied stacks maps cleanly to the primary responsibilities. The main gap is practical Nest.js exposure — a named framework the team relies on daily — which would require an honest ramp-up assessment; the absence of Vitest and Playwright in his toolkit is a secondary but real concern given the testing requirements. The credential anomaly around the MIT listing is worth a brief clarifying question but does not materially change the assessment given the strength of his professional record.
